headlines.title: UK and US always find ways to come together, King to tell Congress
headlines.description: King Charles will address the US Congress on Tuesday at a time of diplomatic tensions between London and Washington.

headlines.title: BP profits more than double as Iran war sends oil prices higher
headlines.description: The energy giant said it had seen an "exceptional" performance at its oil trading business.

headlines.title: Jimmy Kimmel rejects White House criticism over Melania widow joke
headlines.description: In a parody aired days before the White House Correspondents' Dinner, Kimmel called Melania an "expectant widow".

headlines.title: Russian superyacht sails through Strait of Hormuz despite blockade
headlines.description: The 141m-long vessel, linked to a close ally of Russian President Vladimir Putin, cleared the waterway despite an ongoing blockade.

headlines.title: Antiquities dealer who exposed British Museum thefts dies aged 61
headlines.description: Dr Ittai Gradel made global headlines after exposing the thefts of many artefacts from the British Museum.

headlines.title: How armed guards almost seized David Attenborough's iconic gorilla moment
headlines.description: Two new documentaries explore the the fascinating tale behind the defining image of Attenborough's career.

headlines.title: My tenant owes £15,000 in rent, but I can't get them out of the property
headlines.description: Landlords tell BBC News why they fear new laws could make it harder to remove problematic tenants.

headlines.title: Austrian goes on trial accused of plotting attack on Taylor Swift concert
headlines.description: Prosecutors say the man, 21-year-old Beran A, received training from members of jihadist group Islamic State on how to handle explosives.

headlines.title: Figures show rise in suicides after domestic abuse
headlines.description: Police say the increase is driven by improved awareness and a change in recording incidents.

headlines.title: Rebel Wilson says claims she bullied women on her film are 'absolute nonsense'
headlines.description: Rebel Wilson is being sued for defamation over Instagram posts she made about the star of her film The Deb.

headlines.title: I'm proud to have come last at the London Marathon
headlines.description: Clair Martin says she wanted to raise money for the Samaritans, who she credits with saving her life.

headlines.title: In pictures: King Charles and Queen Camilla begin US state visit
headlines.description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla were greeted by a military band and honour guard as they arrived for their four-day state visit.

headlines.title: Key points as MPs question former Foreign Office boss on Mandelson vetting row
headlines.description: Former top Foreign Office civil servant Sir Philip Barton and ex-No 10 chief of staff Morgan McSweeney are answering MPs' questions.

headlines.title: What impact do refugees have on housing in Glasgow?
headlines.description: BBC Verify examines claims made by political leaders about the impact of asylum and migration on housing.

headlines.title: My husband finally got full-time care – he died a week later
headlines.description: Kirsty Parsons shares details of her battle to secure adult social care support for her husband.

headlines.title: I had £20,000 stolen and had to fight a 13-month fraud reporting rule to get it back
headlines.description: Sarah has now got her money back but there are calls to reform the deadline for reporting scams to banks.

headlines.title: Why Spotify has no button to filter out AI music
headlines.description: Music streamer Deezer allows users to filter out AI music, so why does Spotify not offer the same?

headlines.title: 'We might have had more children' - how Wales' childcare costs are hitting families
headlines.description: Parents in Wales say the cost of childcare is one of their biggest worries ahead of the election.

headlines.title: Man, 92, charged with murder after woman found dead at house
headlines.description: Laurence Billam is due in court after the death of 87-year-old Shirley Billam in Exeter.

headlines.title: Northern Ireland to be first part of UK to offer free sign language classes for deaf children
headlines.description: The bill will put a duty on Stormont to provide free classes for deaf people under-25 and their close families, guardians, and carers.

headlines.title: Social media restrictions for under-16s even if no ban, minister says
headlines.description: The government is consulting on changes as a new social media law goes through its final Parliamentary stages.

headlines.title: NZ axes plan for WW2 sex slaves statue after Japan protest
headlines.description: Tokyo had warned that installing the structure in Auckland could jeopardise diplomatic relations.

headlines.title: Family's plea to save dream Disney trip for girl, 6, left blind by brain tumour
headlines.description: Sienna was diagnosed with a tumour just weeks after getting the surprise trip for Christmas.

headlines.title: Valve's £85 Steam Controller divides gamers ahead of May launch
headlines.description: The pad will be compatible with PCs, Valve's handheld console, the Steam Deck as well as its upcoming gaming PC.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: The King Arrives In The US, But Can He Mend The Special Relationship?
headlines.description: The King lands in Washington for four day state visit.

headlines.title: Who is Trump’s 'would-be assassin' and were there security failings at DC shooting?
headlines.description: The US president was evacuated from the White House Correspondents Dinner

headlines.title: All but back in Champions League - yet big decisions loom at Man Utd
headlines.description: Barring an unlikely sequence of results, Old Trafford will host Champions League football again next season, but for those running the club, their most significant work is about to begin.

headlines.title: Ranking the players who could decide Champions League semi-finals
headlines.description: Which players will prove key to their team’s chances in the Champions League last four? We've ranked the top 10.

headlines.title: England's next opener? Durham's Gay stakes his claim
headlines.description: After his match-winning century for Durham, BBC Sport assesses whether Emilio Gay has the skills to be England's next Test opener.

headlines.title: Robertson calls for ban on O'Sullivan's chalk
headlines.description: Former world champion Neil Robertson calls for a controversial chalk used by Ronnie O'Sullivan to be banned, saying most other players "hate it" and that it "makes an absolute mess of the table".

headlines.title: LIV could reschedule June's New Orleans event
headlines.description: LIV Golf could postpone its event in New Orleans in June amid continued questions over its future.

headlines.title: Ineos hope for AI inspiration with Netcompany deal
headlines.description: Ineos Grenadiers agree a lucrative new deal with data business Netcompany, with AI the focus for a return to success.
